page: Amazon Web Services m6i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The configuration provides 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, supported by 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. This setup yields 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data places this instance in the average performance tier for most standard operations. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1664.0 and a multi-core score of 15732.0. In specialized tasks, the CPU demonstrates strong capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for image processing at 15061.0 and Passmark CPU encryption at 43612.59. Memory bandwidth tests show 2503.74 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 161.66 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

While the instance handles lightweight LLM text generation efficiently, scoring in the top 25% with 266.35 tokens/sec on a 135M model, it exhibits a clear performance tradeoff on larger models, dropping to a weak rating of 2.75 tokens/sec for 70B model text generation. This indicates a limitation for heavy AI workloads without GPU acceleration. The resource profile is balanced for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, delivering 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance has no local storage or GPU accelerators, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most compute and memory tasks, with strong results in image processing and CPU encryption. However, a notable tradeoff exists in large-scale machine learning, where text generation for a 70B model drops to a weak 2.75 tokens/second. This instance is configured for balanced workloads such as database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C CPU, delivering balanced performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for general-purpose workloads and databases.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR4 memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Strong performance in image processing and CPU encryption benchmarks
Performance limitations on large-scale LLM text generation
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
